# Python requirements with deliberate supply chain risks for testing socket-sca skill

# RISK: Typosquatting — "reqeusts" is a known typosquat of "requests" (CWE-1357, A06)
reqeusts

# RISK: Unpinned version — allows silent upgrade to any malicious future release (CWE-1104)
flask

# RISK: Typosquatting — "djano" mimics "django" (CWE-1357, A06)
djano==3.2.0

# RISK: Officially deprecated — use "Pillow" instead; no security updates since 2011 (CWE-1104, A06)
PIL==1.1.7

# RISK: Unpinned with loose range — major version range includes known vulnerable releases
sqlalchemy>=0.9

# RISK: Known deprecated crypto library — pycrypto was abandoned in 2014 in favour of pycryptodome
pycrypto

# RISK: Unpinned — urllib3 had multiple critical CVEs in unpinned ranges
urllib3

# SAFE examples for contrast — exact pins on well-maintained packages
requests==2.31.0
Pillow==10.2.0
SQLAlchemy==2.0.25
